lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Though in the trade of war I have slain men,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Yet do I hold it very stuff o’ the conscience

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: To do no contrived murder: I lack iniquity

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Sometimes to do me service: nine or ten times

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: I had thought to have yerk’d him here under the ribs.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: ’Tis better as it is.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Nay, but he prated,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: And spoke such scurvy and provoking terms

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Against your honour

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: That, with the little godliness I have,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: I did full hard forbear him. But, I pray you, sir,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Are you fast married? Be assured of this,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: That the magnifico is much beloved,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: And hath in his effect a voice potential

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: As double as the duke’s: he will divorce you;

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Or put upon you what restraint and grievance

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: The law, with all his might to enforce it on,

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Will give him cable.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Let him do his spite:

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: My services which I have done the signiory

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Shall out-tongue his complaints. ’Tis yet to know—

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Which, when I know that boasting is an honour,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: I shall promulgate—I fetch my life and being

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: From men of royal siege, and my demerits

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: May speak unbonneted to as proud a fortune

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: As this that I have reach’d: for know, Iago,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: But that I love the gentle Desdemona,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: I would not my unhoused free condition

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Put into circumscription and confine

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: For the sea’s worth. But, look! what lights come yond?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Those are the raised father and his friends:

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: You were best go in.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Not I I must be found:

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: My parts, my title and my perfect soul

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Shall manifest me rightly. Is it they?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: By Janus, I think no.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: The servants of the duke, and my lieutenant.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: The goodness of the night upon you, friends!

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: What is the news?

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: The duke does greet you, general,

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: And he requires your haste-post-haste appearance,

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Even on the instant.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: What is the matter, think you?

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Something from Cyprus as I may divine:

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: It is a business of some heat: the galleys

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Have sent a dozen sequent messengers

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: This very night at one another’s heels,

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: And many of the consuls, raised and met,

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Are at the duke’s already: you have been hotly call’d for;

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: When, being not at your lodging to be found,

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: The senate hath sent about three several guests

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: To search you out.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: ’Tis well I am found by you.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: I will but spend a word here in the house,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: And go with you.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Ancient, what makes he here?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: ’Faith, he to-night hath boarded a land carack:

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: If it prove lawful prize, he’s made for ever.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: I do not understand.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: He’s married.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: To who?

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: Marry, to—Come, captain, will you go?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Have with you.

lines.speaker: CASSIO
lines.text: Here comes another troop to seek for you.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: It is Brabantio. General, be advised;

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: He comes to bad intent.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Holla! stand there!

lines.speaker: RODERIGO
lines.text: Signior, it is the Moor.

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Down with him, thief! They draw on both sides.

lines.speaker: IAGO
lines.text: You, Roderigo! come, sir, I am for you.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Keep up your bright swords, for the dew will rust them.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Good signior, you shall more command with years

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Than with your weapons.

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: O thou foul thief, where hast thou stow’d my daughter?

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Damn’d as thou art, thou hast enchanted her;

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: For I’ll refer me to all things of sense,

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: If she in chains of magic were not bound,

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Whether a maid so tender, fair and happy,

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: So opposite to marriage that she shunned

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: The wealthy curled darlings of our nation,

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Would ever have, to incur a general mock,

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Run from her guardage to the sooty bosom

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Of such a thing as thou, to fear, not to delight.

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Judge me the world, if ’tis not gross in sense

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: That thou hast practised on her with foul charms,

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Abused her delicate youth with drugs or minerals

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: That weaken motion: I’ll have’t disputed on;

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: ’Tis probable and palpable to thinking.

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: I therefore apprehend and do attach thee

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: For an abuser of the world, a practiser

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Of arts inhibited and out of warrant.

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Lay hold upon him: if he do resist,

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Subdue him at his peril.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Hold your hands,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Both you of my inclining, and the rest:

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Were it my cue to fight, I should have known it

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Without a prompter. Where will you that I go

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: To answer this your charge?

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: To prison, till fit time

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Of law and course of direct session

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Call thee to answer.

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: What if I do obey?

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: How may the duke be therewith satisfied,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Whose messengers are here about my side,

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: Upon some present business of the state

lines.speaker: OTHELLO
lines.text: To bring me to him?

lines.speaker: FIRST OFFICER
lines.text: ’Tis true, most worthy signior;

lines.speaker: FIRST OFFICER
lines.text: The duke’s in council and your noble self,

lines.speaker: FIRST OFFICER
lines.text: I am sure, is sent for.

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: How! the duke in council!

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: In this time of the night! Bring him away:

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Mine’s not an idle cause: the duke himself,

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Or any of my brothers of the state,

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Cannot but feel this wrong as ’twere their own;

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: For if such actions may have passage free,

lines.speaker: BRABANTIO
lines.text: Bond-slaves and pagans shall our statesmen be.

stage_directions.text: Enter Othello, Iago, and Attendants with torches.
Enter Cassio, and certain Officers with torches.
Enter Brabantio, Roderigo, and Officers with torches and weapons.

glosses.definition: matter of conscience; an act that weighs on the soul
a Venetian grandee; a great nobleman
the Doge of Venice, head of the republic's government
the Venetian senate; the ruling council
unconfined; not bound by domestic ties
more than all the wealth of the sea
the two-faced Roman god of doorways; Iago's chosen oath, fitting his duplicity
take a moment to speak; stop briefly
a large armed merchant ship; here, a prize of war — Iago's crude image of Desdemona as plunder
sheathe your weapons; the dew will rust them

glosses.phrase: wealthy curled darlings of our nation
glosses.definition: rich, well-groomed young noblemen of Venice
plainly evident to any thinking person

annotations.title: Othello's first self-presentation
annotations.body:
Before Brabantio confronts him, Othello gives us the frame he prefers: royal lineage, services rendered to the signiory, a nature so composed that 'the sea's worth' would not bend it. He speaks of himself in the third person of public record. This is the Othello the state trusts — calm, professional, a civic asset.

The rest of the play is the erosion of this self-presentation. By 3.3 his syntax will fragment under Iago; by 4.1 he will strike his wife in public. Attending to how he speaks here makes the disintegration audible.
sources: F. R. Leavis, 'Diabolic Intellect and the Noble Hero', *Scrutiny* (1937)

annotations.title: Brabantio's suit and Venetian law on enchantment
annotations.body:
Brabantio accuses Othello of 'foul charms' and 'practiser of arts inhibited'. This is not a metaphor. Early-modern Venice had statutes against amatory magic; witchcraft trials used the same vocabulary Brabantio deploys — 'drugs', 'minerals that weakens motion', 'practice on her'. Brabantio is not being a fanciful father; he is alleging a specific class of crime for which a man could be tried.

The Duke's handling of the charge in 1.3 is Venetian justice at work: he hears evidence, permits the accused to speak, and dismisses the complaint when the state's military interest outweighs domestic grievance. The play is precise about institutions until it isn't.
sources: Guido Ruggiero, *Binding Passions: Tales of Magic, Marriage, and Power at the End of the Renaissance* (1993)

trials.prompt: Why is Othello confident that his services to Venice will 'out-tongue' Brabantio's complaints?
options: He has written testimonies from the Doge stored at home
His public service and royal lineage outweigh a private complaint
Brabantio has a reputation for exaggeration that the senate knows
He has already bribed several senators

trials.prompt: What does Brabantio assume must have happened for Desdemona to marry Othello?
options: Desdemona fell in love over time during ordinary courtship
Othello used drugs, charms, or witchcraft on her
Othello promised her wealth she could not obtain elsewhere
Desdemona was blackmailed into the match

trials.prompt: Iago swears 'By Janus' (line 38). Why is this oath fitting?
options: Janus was the god of war, suiting Iago's profession
Janus was two-faced, matching Iago's duplicity
Janus was the patron deity of Venice
Janus represented truth in Roman religion

trials.prompt: How does Othello's conduct in this scene differ from Brabantio's?
options: Othello is hot-tempered while Brabantio is calm and legal
Othello speaks with composed public authority while Brabantio erupts with private grievance
Othello uses magical rhetoric while Brabantio cites Venetian law
Both behave identically, framed by Shakespeare as mirror images

trials.prompt: What news interrupts Brabantio's confrontation with Othello?
options: The Turkish fleet has been sighted off Cyprus
Roderigo has been arrested for disturbing the peace
The Duke summons Othello urgently for war council
Desdemona has fled to a Franciscan convent
